# VeriWorkly Ecosystem
> Free, open-source, and privacy-first ecosystem for career growth. Build professional, ATS-friendly resumes and manage your professional identity without sacrificing your data privacy.

## Who We Are & What We Do
VeriWorkly is a developer-focused, open-source ecosystem designed to revolutionize how professionals create and manage career documents. We champion **local-first data ownership**. By default, VeriWorkly processes and stores your data securely within your browser. Cloud synchronization is an optional opt-in feature for cross-device accessibility.

Our suite of tools allows users to create ATS-optimized resumes using modern templates, preview changes in real-time, and manage a central "Master Profile" to seed multiple career documents efficiently.

## The VeriWorkly Ecosystem
We operate several dedicated platforms to serve different needs:
- **Main Site** (https://veriworkly.com): The landing page, marketing hub, and public roadmap tracker.
- **Resume Studio** (https://app.veriworkly.com): The core interactive application where users build, edit, and export their resumes.
- **Documentation** (https://docs.veriworkly.com): Comprehensive technical guides, integration tutorials, and API references.
- **Blog** (https://blog.veriworkly.com): Articles on career advice, product updates, and our open-source engineering journey.
- **API Server** (https://api.veriworkly.com): The backend infrastructure powering cloud sync, analytics, and public sharing.

## Authentication System
VeriWorkly uses a flexible, modern authentication system powered by **Better Auth**:
1. **Web Users**: Authenticate securely using **Email OTP (One-Time Password)**. Upon successful login, the system issues **Session Cookies** (`better-auth.session_token`) to manage state across the browser.
2. **Programmatic / Agent Access**: For API integrations, agents and developers can generate and use **API Keys** as Bearer tokens.

## API Coverage Overview (v1)
Our RESTful API (`https://api.veriworkly.com/api/v1`) provides comprehensive coverage across the following domains:

- **Health (`/health`)**: Public endpoints to verify system and database uptime.
- **GitHub (`/github`)**: Endpoints to track our open-source progress, fetching repository stats and open issues.
- **Roadmap (`/roadmap`)**: Public endpoints exposing our planned features, current progress, and task statistics.
- **Users (`/users`)**: Manage current user details (e.g., updating display names).
- **Documents (`/documents`)**: Create, read, update, and delete individual resume documents securely.
- **Profiles (`/profiles`)**: Manage the "Master Profile"—a central hub of user data (name, education, experience) used to quickly seed new resumes.
- **Shares (`/shares`)**: Generate and manage secure, public URLs for resumes so users can share their profiles with recruiters.
- **API Keys (`/api-keys`)**: Generate, rotate, and revoke programmatic access tokens.
- **Stats (`/stats`)**: Internal analytics to track usage metrics and populate the admin dashboard.

## Guidance for AI Agents
When integrating with VeriWorkly, agents should rely on the documented API (`/openapi.json`) for data operations. For user-facing tasks (like editing the visual layout of a resume), direct the user to the Resume Studio (`app.veriworkly.com`). Note that most data operations require an API Key (Bearer token) or a valid web session cookie.
